# pr-prism

> Filter the noise. Focus on what matters.

Stateful GitHub PR review scraper for AI agent workflows. Fetches review comments via the GitHub GraphQL API, filters bots and noise, emits only new/unresolved comments вЂ” once per ID, forever cached. After fixes are pushed, resolves threads and tags AI agents for re-review.

## Commands

- `pnpm run pr-review` вЂ” detect repo, list open PRs, interactive select
- `pnpm run pr-review -- 42` вЂ” process PR #42 directly (non-interactive)
- `pnpm run pr-review -- <url>` вЂ” process by full GitHub PR URL
- `pnpm run pr-resolve` вЂ” resolve threads from latest sidecar (auto-runs pr-review if missing)
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42` вЂ” explicit PR number
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --dry-run` вЂ” preview without mutating
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --tag-agents` вЂ” resolve + post @mention comment
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --tag-agents --comment "Fixed in abc123"` вЂ” custom message
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --unresolve` вЂ” re-open resolved threads
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --auto` вЂ” smart auto-resolve: re-fetch threads, resolve only addressed ones
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --auto --dry-run` вЂ” preview auto-resolve classifications
- `pnpm run pr-resolve -- 42 --auto --tag-agents` вЂ” auto-resolve + tag agents

## Output Files

- `pr-reviews/new-<timestamp>.md` вЂ” new actionable comments since last run
- `pr-reviews/.scraped-ids.json` вЂ” persistent ID cache (commit this file)
- `pr-reviews/.threads-<pr>.json` вЂ” thread IDs consumed by pr-resolve

## Comment Format (in output markdown)

Each comment header includes visible IDs for LLM reference:

```
## рџ’¬ **author** В· thread `PRRT_xxx` В· `#databaseId`   в†ђ first comment of an inline thread
## рџ’¬ **author** В· `#databaseId`                        в†ђ subsequent comments / general comments
```

## What Gets Filtered

- Already-seen comment IDs (in `.scraped-ids.json`) вЂ” silently skipped
- Resolved threads (`isResolved: true`) вЂ” skipped and cached
- Bot authors (ending in `[bot]` or matching `KNOWN_BOTS`) вЂ” skipped and cached
- Outdated threads (`isOutdated: true`) вЂ” shown with `### вљ пёЏ OUTDATED / SUPERSEDED`
- Social sharing links (Twitter, Reddit, LinkedIn, CodeAnt) вЂ” stripped from body

## Config (`.pr-prism.json` in repo root)

Create this file to customise behaviour without editing source:

```json
{
  "agentMentions": ["cubic-dev-ai", "coderabbitai"]
}
```

`agentMentions` вЂ” list of GitHub handles (without `@`) tagged in the post-resolve comment. Defaults to `["cubic-dev-ai"]`.

## The AI Agent Loop

```
1. pnpm run pr-review -- <PR number>
        в†’ pr-reviews/new-<timestamp>.md   (new comments only)
        в†’ pr-reviews/.threads-<pr>.json   (thread IDs for resolve)

2. Agent reads markdown, implements fixes, commits, pushes

3. pnpm run pr-resolve -- <PR number> --auto --tag-agents
        в†’ Re-fetches live thread state from GitHub
        в†’ Auto-resolves: isOutdated threads + matched suggestions
        в†’ Posts: "Auto-resolved 6/8 threads. 2 remain."
        в†’ Tags agents for re-review

4. Repeat вЂ” only new reviewer replies appear
```

## Requirements

- Node.js в‰Ґ 20
- gh CLI authenticated (`gh auth login`)

## Source

- [README](./README.md) вЂ” full documentation
- [scripts/scrape-pr-reviews.ts](./scripts/scrape-pr-reviews.ts) вЂ” scraper
- [scripts/resolve-pr-threads.ts](./scripts/resolve-pr-threads.ts) вЂ” resolver
- [agents/scrape-pr-reviews.md](./agents/scrape-pr-reviews.md) вЂ” agent skill reference
